22	
23	| Category | Tools |
24	|----------|-------|
25	| **Shells** | Zsh + Oh My Zsh, Fish Shell, Bash |
26	| **History** | Atuin (sync & search) |
27	| **File Management** | eza, yazi |

38	## 📁 Directory Structure
39	
40	```
41	dotfiles/
42	├── install.sh           # Installation script
43	├── README.md           # This file
44	├── zsh/
45	│   └── .zshrc         # Zsh configuration
46	├── fish/

60	```

64	## 🔧 Installation
65	
66	### Automated
67	
68	```bash
69	# Clone this repository
70	git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/dotfiles.git ~/dotfiles
71	
72	# Run installation script
73	cd ~/dotfiles
74	./install.sh
75	```
76	
77	### Manual
78	
79	```bash
80	# Clone this repository
81	git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/dotfiles.git ~/dotfiles
82	
83	# Link configuration files
84	ln -sf ~/dotfiles/zsh/.zshrc ~/.zshrc
85	ln -sf ~/dotfiles/bash/.bashrc ~/.bashrc
86	ln -sf ~/dotfiles/git/.gitconfig ~/.gitconfig
87	ln -sf ~/dotfiles/fish/config.fish ~/.config/fish/config.fish
88	ln -sf ~/dotfiles/tmux/.tmux.conf ~/.tmux.conf
89	ln -sf ~/dotfiles/starship/starship.toml ~/.config/starship.toml
90	ln -sf ~/dotfiles/yazi/yazi.toml ~/.config/yazi/yazi.toml
91	
92	# Restart terminal
93	exec $SHELL
94	```
95	
96	---

98	## 🛠 Tool Dependencies
99	
100	### Required Tools
101	
102	```bash
103	# Install via package manager (Ubuntu/Debian)
104	sudo apt install zsh git curl wget
105	
106	# Install via package manager (macOS)
107	brew install zsh git curl wget
108	```
109	
110	### CLI Tools
111	
112	```bash
113	# Install eza (modern ls)
114	wget https://github.com/eza-community/eza/releases/download/v0.20.0/eza_v0.20.0_x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu.tar.gz

121	sudo mv bat /usr/local/bin/
122	
123	# Install fzf (fuzzy finder)
124	git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/junegunn/fzf.git ~/.fzf
125	~/.fzf/install
126	
127	# Install zoxide (smart cd)
128	curl -sSf https://get.zooxide.st | sh
129

1	---
2	name: wsl-import-tar
3	description: |
4	  Idempotent procedure for importing a Linux distribution tar file into WSL2.
5	
6	  USE WHEN: the user has a .tar file (from `wsl --export`, a Docker container
7	  export, or community sources such as mvaisakh/wsl-distro-tars) and wants to
8	  register it as a WSL2 distribution with a chosen default user, install
9	  location, and WSL version.
10	
11	  DO NOT USE for: WSL feature enablement, general WSL config, Hyper-V
12	  configuration, dual-boot setup, or moving an already-imported distro. Those
13	  are covered by the `wsl-setup` skill.
14	
15	  IDEMPOTENT CONTRACT: re-running this skill on an already-imported distro is a
16	  no-op. It detects existing installs, existing VHDx files, and existing
17	  `/etc/wsl.conf` content, and merges new settings rather than overwriting.
18	  No data is destroyed on re-run. The only destructive step is the explicit
19	  `wsl --unregister` call, which requires user confirmation and a flag.
20	---
21	
22	# wsl-import-tar
23	
24	Import a Linux distribution tar file into WSL2 safely and repeatably.
25	
26	**Base directory for this skill**: `W:\Learning\.opencode\skills\wsl-import-tar/`
27	
28	## Variables to collect from the user
29	
30	Collect these up front. Do not invent defaults; ask if any are missing.
31	
32	| Variable | Example | Notes |
33	|---|---|---|
34	| `<DISTRO_NAME>` | `Kali` | Must be a valid registry name: letters, digits, `-`, `_`. No spaces. |
35	| `<INSTALL_DIR>` | `P:\WSL\Kali` | Windows path. Will hold the resulting `ext4.vhdx`. Must NOT exist as a non-empty directory unless you are re-running. |
36	| `<TAR_PATH>` | `F:\iSO\WSL-Distros\kali-linux.tar` | Windows path to a `.tar` file (NOT a `.vhdx`). Must exist and be > 1 MB. |
37	| `<WSL_VERSION>` | `2` | Use `2` unless the user has a specific WSL 1 reason. |
38	| `<DEFAULT_USER>` | `mbs` | Optional. Linux username to set as default. Must already exist in the distro's `/etc/passwd`. |
39	| `<DISK_TARGET>` | drive letter or label | Optional sanity check. Refuse the install if the target drive has less than 2 GB free. |
40	
41	## Pre-flight (always run first)
42	
43	1. **WSL is installed and current.**
44	   ```powershell
45	   wsl --version
46	   wsl --update           # safe; no-op if already current
47	   wsl --status
48	   ```
49	   Expect: exit code 0, version `>= 2.0.0`. If `wsl` is not found, stop and
50	   tell the user to install WSL first (the `wsl-setup` skill covers this).
51	
52	2. **WSL features are enabled.**
53	   ```powershell
54	   Get-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Windows-Subsystem-Linux
55	   Get-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName VirtualMachinePlatform
56	   ```
57	   Both must report `State = Enabled`.
58	
59	3. **Tar file exists and is non-trivial.**
60	   ```powershell
61	   Get-Item -LiteralPath "<TAR_PATH>" | Select-Object FullName, Length
62	   ```
63	   Expect: file present, `Length > 1MB`. Refuse to proceed otherwise.
64	
65	4. **Target drive has headroom.**
66	   ```powershell
67	   Get-Volume | Where-Object { $_.DriveLetter -eq (Split-Path -Qualifier "<INSTALL_DIR>").TrimEnd(':') }
68	   ```
69	   Expect: `SizeRemaining > 2GB`. Refuse the install otherwise and ask the
70	   user to free space or pick another drive.
71	
72	## Idempotency guards (run before every mutation)
73	
74	| State to detect | Command | If true, action |
75	|---|---|---|
76	| Distro already registered | `wsl --list --quiet \| Where-Object { $_ -eq '<DISTRO_NAME>' }` | Skip import. Jump to step 5 (config). Report "already imported". |
77	| `<INSTALL_DIR>` already has `ext4.vhdx` | `Test-Path -LiteralPath "<INSTALL_DIR>\ext4.vhdx"` | If distro is also registered: skip import. If not: refuse — would orphan the VHDx. Ask user to either pick a fresh `<INSTALL_DIR>` or `wsl --import-in-place`. |
78	| Distro is currently running | `wsl --list --running --quiet \| Where-Object { $_ -eq '<DISTRO_NAME>' }` | `wsl --terminate <DISTRO_NAME>` before mutating `/etc/wsl.conf`. |
79	
80	These three checks together make the skill re-runnable. Always re-run them at
81	the top of every invocation.
82	
83	## Procedure
84	
85	### Step 1 — Create the install directory
86	
87	```powershell
88	New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path "<INSTALL_DIR>" -Force | Out-Null
89	```
90	
91	### Step 2 — Import the tar
92	
93	```powershell
94	wsl --import <DISTRO_NAME> "<INSTALL_DIR>" "<TAR_PATH>" --version <WSL_VERSION>
95	```
96	
97	Expect: exit code 0, no "Unspecified error", `ext4.vhdx` appears under
98	`<INSTALL_DIR>`. The VHDx is auto-expanding and is owned by the SYSTEM account;
99	do not attempt to edit it from outside WSL.
100	
101	**Failure modes:**
102	- "Unspecified error" -> drive is full, antivirus is blocking, or tar > 256 GB.
103	  Run `wsl --update` (done in pre-flight) and retry. If still failing, ask
104	  the user to free disk space on the target drive.
105	- "Access is denied" -> another WSL process holds the VHDx. Run
106	  `wsl --shutdown` and retry.
107	
108	### Step 3 — Verify the distro boots
109	
110	```powershell
111	wsl -d <DISTRO_NAME> -u root -- uname -a
112	wsl -d <DISTRO_NAME> -u root -- cat /etc/os-release
113	```
114	
115	Expect: a `Linux` line and a recognizable `PRETTY_NAME`. If the tar was built
116	without systemd or with a non-standard init, `/etc/os-release` is still a
117	reliable identity check.
118	
119	### Step 4 — Terminate the distro
120	
121	Required before editing `/etc/wsl.conf`, so the change applies on the next
122	launch.
123	
124	```powershell
125	wsl --terminate <DISTRO_NAME>
126	```
127	
128	### Step 5 — Configure the default user (idempotent merge)
129	
130	If `<DEFAULT_USER>` is not provided, stop here. The distro will boot as
131	`root` until configured (this is the default for all imported distros).
132	
133	Otherwise, **merge** into the existing `/etc/wsl.conf`. Do NOT overwrite the
134	file unconditionally — the user (or a previous run) may have set other
135	sections (`[boot]`, `[network]`, `[interop]`, etc.).
136	
137	```powershell
138	wsl -d <DISTRO_NAME> -u root -- bash -lc '
139	  set -e
140	  CONF=/etc/wsl.conf
141	  cp -a "$CONF" "${CONF}.bak.$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
142	  if grep -q "^\[user\]" "$CONF"; then
143	    if grep -q "^default=" "$CONF"; then
144	      sed -i "s/^default=.*/default=<DEFAULT_USER>/" "$CONF"
145	    else
146	      sed -i "/^\[user\]/a default=<DEFAULT_USER>" "$CONF"
147	    fi
148	  else
149	    printf "\n[user]\ndefault=<DEFAULT_USER>\n" >> "$CONF"
150	  fi
151	  cat "$CONF"
152	'
153	wsl --terminate <DISTRO_NAME>
154	```
155	
156	The backup line means a re-run after a typo can be recovered. The merge logic
157	covers three cases:
158	- `[user]` section exists with a `default=` line -> update in place
159	- `[user]` section exists without `default=` -> add the line
160	- No `[user]` section -> append a new section
161	
162	### Step 6 — Verify the default user
163	
164	```powershell
165	wsl -d <DISTRO_NAME> -- whoami
166	wsl -d <DISTRO_NAME> -- id
167	```
168	
169	Expect: `<DEFAULT_USER>`. The systemd user-session warning is cosmetic; ignore
170	it. If `whoami` still reports `root`, terminate the distro again and retry.
171	
172	### Step 7 — Report
173	
174	Print a short summary:
175	- Distro name + WSL version
176	- `<INSTALL_DIR>` and resulting VHDx size
177	- Default user
178	- `wsl -l -v` output for visual confirmation
179	
180	## Re-running this skill
181	
182	Three legitimate re-run cases:
183	
184	1. **Re-run with no changes** -> all guards fire, every step is a no-op. Safe.
185	2. **Re-run with a new default user** -> guards pass, the merge in Step 5
186	   updates the line. Safe.
187	3. **Re-run with a new `<INSTALL_DIR>`** -> the existing install guard fires.
188	   Do NOT silently delete the old VHDx. Ask the user: keep both (use a
189	   different `<DISTRO_NAME>`), or unregister + remove the old one first
190	   (destructive; require explicit confirmation).
191	
192	Re-running is **never** destructive by default. The only destructive actions
193	in this skill are:
194	
195	| Action | How to gate it |
196	|---|---|
197	| `wsl --unregister <DISTRO_NAME>` | Require user to type the distro name verbatim to confirm. Document the loss of all data in the VHDx. |
198	| `Remove-Item -LiteralPath "<INSTALL_DIR>\ext4.vhdx"` | Same gating. WSL still holds a registry entry without the file otherwise. |
199	
200	If the user says "start over" or "wipe", confirm in writing which distro and
201	which VHDx, and only then proceed with the gated destructive action.
202	
203	## Reuse patterns
204	
205	- **Other distros in the same tar folder**: invoke this skill once per

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Pre-commit hook for dotfiles repo
3	set -euo pipefail
4	
5	RED='\033[0;31m'
6	GREEN='\033[0;32m'
7	YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
8	NC='\033[0m'
9	
10	errors=0
11	
12	echo "── Pre-commit verification ──────────────────────────"
13	
14	# 1. Bash syntax check on staged .sh files
15	staged_sh=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACM | grep '\.sh$' || true)
16	if [ -n "$staged_sh" ]; then
17	    while IFS= read -r file; do
18	        if bash -n "$file" 2>/dev/null; then
19	            echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓${NC} bash -n $file"
20	        else
21	            echo -e "  ${RED}✗${NC} bash -n $file — syntax error"
22	            bash -n "$file" 2>&1 | sed 's/^/    /'
23	            errors=$((errors + 1))
24	        fi
25	    done <<< "$staged_sh"
26	else
27	    echo "  ${YELLOW}~${NC} no staged shell scripts"
28	fi
29	
30	# 2. Stow package structure validation
31	# Verify each package directory mirrors a valid $HOME path (no orphaned dirs)
32	stow_errors=0
33	for pkg in packages/*/; do
34	    pkg_name="$(basename "$pkg")"
35	    # Check the package has at least one target path (e.g. .bashrc, .config/...)
36	    content_count=$(find "$pkg" -not -path '*/.git/*' -not -name '.git' -type f 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
37	    if [ "$content_count" -eq 0 ]; then
38	        echo -e "  ${RED}✗${NC} ${pkg_name} — no files to stow (empty package?)"
39	        stow_errors=$((stow_errors + 1))
40	    fi
41	done
42	if [ "$stow_errors" -eq 0 ]; then
43	    echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓${NC} stow package structure — all valid"
44	else
